addDevice.title: Add another device
addDevice.subtitle: Use the same identity on a second device. The transfer happens locally — no server sees your key.

comparison.transfersTitle: What follows you to the new device

transfers.identityDetail: Both devices sign as the same member.
transfers.balanceDetail: Visible on both devices — these live in the community ledger, not on your device.

stays.dmsDetail: DMs are end-to-end encrypted to this device. The new device starts fresh.
stays.draftsDetail: Local-only on purpose. Finish or copy anything you don't want to lose first.
stays.themeDensityDetail: Per-device on purpose — your eyes, not your identity.
stays.achievementsDetail: These rebuild on the new device once it syncs with the community.

gate.title: Heads up: this QR is your identity for 5 minutes.
gate.body1: Security cameras, doorbell cams, and webcams can read QR codes from across a room. Once it's on screen, anyone in camera view can save it and replay it within the next 5 minutes — even after you close this screen.
gate.body2: With the passphrase, that gives them everything: your name, your history, the ability to sign as you.
gate.commitment: Do this in a room you control. If you're not sure, cancel.

display.spokenInstructions: Read these six words aloud, or type them on the new device. The order matters. Don't paste — clipboard apps remember things.
display.afterImport: When the other device says it's done, tap Done. The QR will also clear on its own when the timer reaches zero.
display.fingerprintAriaLabel: Identity fingerprint to confirm on the other device

expired.title: Time's up.
expired.body: Five minutes passed. The QR is gone and so is the passphrase. If you still want to pair the other device, start over — a fresh passphrase will be generated.

errors.title: Couldn't start pairing
errors.locked: Unlock your session before pairing — your identity key is sealed.

labelSource.title: Done. Want to label this for your records?
labelSource.body: A short note — like "Aunt's laptop" or "work phone" — helps you recognize this entry later in your Profile. The label stays on this device.

pairDevice.title: Pair this device
pairDevice.subtitle: Scan the QR from your other device, then type the 6 words it's showing.

directions.title: Need a QR? Get one from your other device.
directions.step1: On the other device, open Profile, then "Add another device."
directions.step2: Follow the prompts — the QR appears with a 6-word code below it.
directions.step3: Hold the other device's screen up to this camera. It scans automatically. Or paste the QR contents into the box below if the camera can't see it.

capture.cameraInstructions: Point at the QR on the other device. It'll detect automatically.
capture.cameraUnavailableHelp: Your browser doesn't have a built-in QR reader, or you didn't allow camera access. You can paste the QR contents instead, if the other device offered them.
capture.videoAriaLabel: Live camera view, looking for a QR code

passphrase.instructions: Type the {{count}} words from the other device, in order. They auto-complete as you type.

fingerprintConfirm.title: Does this match the other device?
fingerprintConfirm.body: The other device should be showing the same 8-character code under "Confirm on the other device." If it doesn't match, stop — something is wrong with the scan.
fingerprintConfirm.fingerprintAriaLabel: Identity fingerprint from the device you scanned
fingerprintConfirm.mismatchHint: If you tap "No" we'll restart the scan on this device. Ask the other device to start over too.

session.title: Lock this device with a passphrase
session.intro: Pick a passphrase to protect your identity on this device. You'll type it when the app asks you to unlock. Leave it blank to skip locking on this device — only do that if no one else has access.
session.skipHint: Leave both blank to skip — your key stays unwrapped on this device.

bootstrap.title: You're in.
bootstrap.intro: A few things this device starts without:

bullets.noDms: No DM history — messages are end-to-end encrypted to whichever device received them.
bullets.noDrafts: No drafts — the other device may have unsaved posts; those don't follow.
bullets.noPrefs: No theme or density preferences — set them in Profile → Settings if you want them to match.

bootstrap.outro: Achievements rebuild on their own as this device syncs with the community.

errors.lostEnvelope: The QR data is missing. Capture it again.
errors.malformed: That doesn't look like a pairing QR. Capture it again.
errors.wrongPassphrase: These words don't match. Check each one — order matters.
errors.expired: This transfer is past its 5-minute window. Ask the other device to start over.
errors.versionMismatch: The other device is on a different version. Update both, then try again.
errors.publickeyMismatch: The QR's identity check didn't add up. Ask the other device to start over.

labelDestination.title: Want to label this device for your records?
labelDestination.body: A short note — like "my laptop" or "travel phone" — shows up on your Profile so you can see what's paired. The label stays on this device.

offline.banner: You're offline. Everything here still works from your device.
offline.pendingCount_one: {{count}} change will send when you reconnect.
offline.pendingCount_other: {{count}} changes will send when you reconnect.

toast.needPosted: Posted. Your need is live on the board.
toast.offerPosted: Posted. Your offer is live on the board.
toast.projectCreated: Project created. Add tasks and launch it when you're ready.
toast.exchangeConfirmedPending: Confirmed. Waiting for the other person to confirm too.
toast.exchangeConfirmedComplete: Confirmed. Credits have flowed between you.

autoConfirmHours.label: Auto-confirm after
autoConfirmHours.help: When one party stops responding, the community node's system key closes out the exchange so credit can flow. Set to 0 to disable auto-confirm entirely. Requires the operator to have a system key configured; without one the sweep is a no-op.

common.copied: Copied. Share it over Signal, in person, or on paper.
common.copyFailed: Couldn't access the clipboard — select the link above and copy manually.

notFound.title: This page isn't here
notFound.message: The link may be old or mistyped. Nothing's wrong on your end.

drafts.bannerMessage: You started one of these {{when}}. Continue where you left off, or start fresh?

profileNudge.label: Profile completion
profileNudge.message: Members can find you for in-person help if you add a neighborhood or a skill or two. Optional, but it helps.
profileNudge.technical: Profile fields visible on your posts and member detail: skills (free-text comma-separated tokens, no taxonomy), zone (coarsened to neighborhood — never street address — so geographic context doesn't leak past what you explicitly shared), availability free-text notes, availability chips (coarse buckets — weekday days, weekday evenings, weekend days, weekend evenings, ask me anytime). The nudge dismisses when any of these is filled, or on explicit dismissal. Dismissal is per-device, not federated, and clears on hard purge.

firstActionNudge.label: Getting started
firstActionNudge.message: Two ways in: tap any post that catches your eye to see how exchange works, or share something you need or can offer. No pressure to post first — lurking is welcome too.
firstActionNudge.technical: The nudge appears on the Board until you take a first action — claiming a post, posting your own need or offer, or explicitly dismissing it. Once any of these happens, it stays dismissed per-device. A hard reset (Profile → Emergency → Hard purge) or a fresh install brings the nudge back; the state lives in local IndexedDB, not on the community node.

vouchNudge.label: Vouching is open to you
vouchNudge.title: You can vouch now
vouchNudge.body: Someone in the community vouched for you. You can do the same for people you trust — it's a way to expand who the community sees as trustworthy. Visit any member's profile and tap Vouch.

keepAccessNudge.label: Keeping your account safe
keepAccessNudge.title: Keep a spare copy of your account
keepAccessNudge.body: Your account lives on this device — that's what keeps it private. If you have a second device, pair it: your phone and a laptop can hold the same account, so losing one doesn't lock you out. It only takes a minute, and you do it with both devices in front of you.
keepAccessNudge.learnMore: There's no password to reset and no company that can restore your account — that's the trade for having no one able to lock you out or hand you over. A second paired device is the way to keep access if a device is ever lost. (Saving your data export or setting a passphrase protects different things; neither one brings your account back on a new device.)

card.label: Add Understoria to your phone
card.title: Keep Understoria one tap away
card.iosHint: Tap Share, then "Add to Home Screen".
card.androidHint: Open your browser's menu, then "Add to Home screen".
card.desktopHint: Click the install icon in your address bar.

panel.title: Add Understoria to your phone
panel.intro: Add Understoria to your home screen and it opens like an app — full screen, offline-ready, no app store account.

oneTap.done: Added. You can open Understoria from your home screen now.

ios.intro: In Safari, add Understoria to your home screen:
ios.step1: Tap the Share button at the bottom of the screen.
ios.step2: Scroll down and tap "Add to Home Screen".
ios.step3: Tap "Add" in the top corner. Understoria appears with your other apps.

iosOther.title: Open this in Safari
iosOther.body: On an iPhone or iPad, only Safari can add an app to your home screen. Open this page in Safari, then add it from there.

android.intro: Add Understoria to your home screen:

desktop.step1: Look for the install icon at the right end of the address bar (Chrome or Edge).
desktop.step3: Understoria opens in its own window, with your other apps.

inAppBrowser.title: Open this in your browser first
inAppBrowser.body: You're viewing Understoria inside another app, which can't add it to your home screen. Open this page in Safari or Chrome, then add it from there.
inAppBrowser.hint: Look for "Open in browser" in this app's menu (often the ⋯ or share icon).

step.title: Add Understoria to your phone
step.intro: Optional, but handy: add Understoria to your home screen so it opens like an app and works offline. You can always do this later from Profile → Learn.

board.label: Board orientation
board.message: Three tabs at the top: Needs (what people are asking for), Offers (what people can share), and Projects (group goals). Tap a post to see more or to claim it. The Board hides posts someone's already claimed, so you see what still needs help.
board.technical: Each tab filters the same post stream by intent. Tapping a post opens its detail view where you can claim it, reach out to the poster, or — if you're the claimant — confirm completion to release credits. Claimed posts are filtered from the default Board view to reduce noise; the filter resets each load. Posts from peer communities (federated via the community node) show a small chip and follow the same lifecycle, but credits don't flow between nodes — each community's ledger is sovereign.

balance.label: Understanding your balance
balance.message: Your balance is hours of help. Everyone starts with 5 so you can ask for help right away, even before you've given any. One hour of any kind of help equals one hour. Give and receive at your own pace.
balance.technical: Balances are time credits, denominated in hours, kept in your community's local ledger. New members receive a 5-hour seed grant when they join so participation isn't credit-gated — the 'asking first is fine' principle. Credits flow only on a fully-confirmed exchange: both parties sign the completed-exchange record (Ed25519) and the local node updates both balances atomically. One hour equals one hour regardless of category — childcare, tech support, carpentry, emotional labor all settle at par. Balances do not federate across nodes by design; cross-community exchanges still happen but the credit settles in the helper's home node.

invite.label: Growing the community
invite.message: Communities grow through trust. When you invite someone, you're vouching for them — your name backs their join. New members need two vouches before they appear as 'trusted' on the Board. Share invites with people you know.
invite.technical: Each invite is a single-use signed token (Ed25519 signature over the invite payload, base64url-encoded) you share out-of-band — Signal, in person, on paper. When the recipient redeems the token, a vouch from you to them is automatically created. Trust thresholds are binary: a member becomes 'trusted' on reaching 2 distinct vouchers (the threshold is a build-time constant — `MINIMUM_VOUCHES_FOR_TRUST` in `lib/vouch.ts`). Vouches are public, signed, immutable — you can issue a manual vouch later but you cannot retract one. If you regret a vouch, the next step is a community conversation, not a code action.

calendar.title: Community calendar
calendar.tagline: What the community is doing together — project deadlines, posts about to expire, and the rhythm of exchanges that already happened.

density.tooltipBody: No member names appear here. The count is the community's metabolism, not anyone's score.

empty.title: The community's calendar is quiet this week.
empty.body: When someone creates a project with a deadline or posts a need that expires, it'll show up here. Activity from completed exchanges shows up too — the calendar reflects what the community is doing together, not a score to chase. Know of something the community should gather for? The + button below starts an event.

templates.title: Pick a starting point
templates.intro: These are starter shapes for common get-togethers. Pick one to fill in the form below — you can edit anything before you sign. Or start from scratch.
templates.selected: Starting from the {{name}} template — edit anything below before you sign.

search.ariaLabel: Search event templates by name or description

filters.empty: No templates match. Try a different search, or start from scratch below.

new.workDayBannerBody: This event will appear on the project page on this node. On peer nodes it appears as a regular community event — the project connection never federates. The title below is yours to edit before you sign.
new.workDayDescriptionPrefill: A hands-on work session for {{project}}. Come for any part of it.
new.title: Create event
new.subtitle: An event is a signed, federated record. Names what you're committing to before you sign.
new.descriptionPlaceholder: What's happening, what to bring, who it's for.
new.locationHint: Free text. Don't put a literal address that you wouldn't publish — every peer node will see this.
new.signingMeansOrganizer: Your public key is published as the organizer. Anyone with federation access can see you organized this.
new.signingMeansPublic: The location and start time you enter are visible on every peer node. Choose a description that matches what you can publish.
new.signingMeansPermanent: Events are append-only. You can cancel; you cannot delete or edit. A cancellation is itself a signed, permanent record.
new.signingMeansRsvp: RSVPs from members on this node are visible to you and to other RSVPs on this node only. Members on other nodes never see the roster.
new.signingNotEditable: Editable — to change the time or location, cancel and re-create.
new.signingNotPrivate: Privately scoped — there is no "members only" event in phase 1. If it shouldn't be on a public wire, coordinate over DM or a NEED/OFFER post instead.
new.errorGeneric: Couldn't create the event. Please try again.
new.errorTitleRequired: Give your event a title so members know what they'd be coming to.
new.errorLocationRequired: Add a location — even a casual one like "the library bench".
new.errorStartRequired: Pick a start date and time.
new.errorStartInPast: Event start time is in the past. Pick a time later than now.
new.errorEndInvalid: End time isn't valid. Pick a date and time, or remove the end-time toggle.
new.errorEndBeforeStart: End time has to be after the start time.
new.errorCapacityInvalid: Capacity must be a positive whole number, or leave it empty for uncapped.
new.errorLocked: Unlock this device with your passphrase before signing the event.

detail.notFound: We couldn't find that event on this node.
detail.cancelHint: Cancellation is the only post-create change in phase 1. The cancellation itself is a signed, permanent record; members who RSVP'd will see the reason on their attention rail.
detail.cancelDialogBody: This signs a cancellation record. It federates to peer nodes and cannot be undone. RSVP'd members will see this on their attention rail.
detail.cancelReasonPlaceholder: Why is this changing? A sentence is plenty.
detail.cancelLocked: Unlock this device with your passphrase before signing the cancellation.
detail.rosterHiddenHint: RSVP "Going" or "Maybe" to see who else is coming. Everyone else sees the count only.

rsvp.heading: RSVP to this event
rsvp.signingBodyGoing: If you choose Going or Maybe, the organizer and other RSVP'd members will see your name on this event.
rsvp.signingBodyNotGoing: If you choose Not going, your choice is private — only you can see it.
rsvp.signingBodyLocalOnly: Your RSVP stays on this node. It is NOT federated to peer nodes.

multiDay.ariaLabelGoing: Event you're going to, day {{index}} of {{count}}
multiDay.ariaLabelGoingFinal: Event you're going to, final day

messages.title: Messages

shell.emptyPane: Pick a conversation from the list.

messages.empty: No messages yet. Start a conversation from any post or member profile.
messages.lockedError: Unlock your session to send messages.
messages.sendError: Message could not be sent. Try again.
messages.noReadReceipts: Messages are private. No read receipts.

projects.empty: No active projects yet. A project is a collective goal — multiple tasks, multiple contributors. The first one is always the hardest to start.

sparkline.label: Project contributions over the last {{days}} days; {{hours}} hours total.
sparkline.caption: Last {{days}} days · {{hours}}h contributed

create.title: Start a project
create.subtitle: A shared goal that needs more than one person and more than one task.
create.fieldTargetHoursHint: Total hours you think this will take, all tasks combined.
create.errorTitle: Give the project a short title.
create.errorHours: Target hours must be a positive number.
create.errorDeadlineDays: Days must be a whole number greater than 0, or blank for no deadline.

templates.title: Pick a starting point
templates.intro: These are starter templates with pre-written tasks. Pick one to fill in the form below — you can edit anything before creating. Or start from scratch.
templates.selected: Starting from the {{name}} template — edit anything below before creating.

search.ariaLabel: Search templates by name or description

filters.empty: No templates match. Try clearing a filter, or start from scratch below.

detail.noTasks: No tasks yet. The organizer can add the first one below.
detail.launchHint: Move from draft to active. Members can claim tasks once you launch.

planningBanner.title: Draft mode
planningBanner.bodyOrganizer: This project is being put together. Members can see it but can't claim tasks yet. Launch it when you're ready to invite hands.
planningBanner.bodyMember: This project is being put together. Tasks become claimable once the organizer launches it.

empty.mine: Nothing claimed by you here right now.

taskDeepLink.filtersCleared: Showing all tasks so the linked task is visible.

workingAlongside.title: Working alongside
workingAlongside.intro: Members who have picked up or finished a task here.

detail.notFound: This task couldn't be found — it may have been removed.

task.claimSummary: About {{hours}} hours · we'll check in with you privately after {{days}} days.
task.claimSummaryNoHours: We'll check in with you privately after {{days}} days.
task.checkInAckToast: Got it — we'll check in again in {{days}} days.
task.releaseReassurance: Releasing helps the organizer find another helper — no judgment.
task.notClaimablePlanning: This project is still planning — the organizer hasn't launched it yet.
task.notClaimablePaused: This project is paused — no new claims right now.
task.notClaimableOther: This project isn't accepting claims right now.
task.claimableAfterLaunch: Members will be able to claim this once you launch the project.

addFreshCopy.hint: Adds an open copy of this task at the bottom of the list — handy for work that recurs.
addFreshCopy.toast: Added a fresh “{{title}}” at the bottom of the task list.

actualHours.hint: Record the real time — the credit should match the help given. No judgment either way.

claimerNarrative.intro: You've marked this done. An organizer confirms to complete the exchange — your {{hours}} hours of credit move then.
claimerNarrative.autoConfirm_one: If no one gets to it, the node confirms automatically within about {{count}} day.
claimerNarrative.autoConfirm_other: If no one gets to it, the node confirms automatically within about {{count}} days.

confirmDialog.title: Confirm this task?
confirmDialog.body: Confirming records a signed exchange: {{hours}} hours move to {{claimer}}, and {{hours}} hours move out of your balance — you're signing as the helped party on behalf of the project.
confirmDialog.estimateNote: {{claimer}} recorded the actual time: {{actual}}. The task was estimated at {{estimate}}.

task.needsMoreHandsTooltip: This task could use additional support. Anyone can step in to help.

acknowledgment.toggle: Add a note for the contributor
acknowledgment.hint: Optional. Visible to the community on the project page.

task.followsHint: This task is ready after the tasks it follows are completed.
task.dragHint: Drag to reorder, or use the Move buttons.
task.dragStart: {{title}} grabbed. Use arrow keys to move, space to drop, Esc to cancel.
task.reorderDialogHint: Drag to reorder. Each change is saved automatically.
task.reorderDialogEmpty: Add at least two tasks to reorder them.
task.waitingOnClaimerNote: You'll be reminded when it's ready.
task.dependsOnHint: Tasks that should complete before this one. Helpful for the attention rail; not a hard block.
task.dependencyCycleError: That would create a circular dependency.

comments.empty: No comments yet. Be the first to say something.
comments.placeholder: Add a comment about this task...
comments.deleteConfirm: Delete this comment? You can't undo this.
comments.flagPrompt: Why are you flagging this comment? (Optional — leave blank if you'd rather the community decide without a reason.)

announcements.title: Updates
announcements.placeholder: Share an update with the community...

completionMoment.title: Complete, together.
completionMoment.summary_one: {{count}} member moved {{hours}} hours to carry this to the finish.
completionMoment.thanksHint: If you'd like to thank everyone, post an update — it stays with the project.

coOrganizers.title: Co-organizers
coOrganizers.intro: Co-organizers can confirm tasks, add tasks, and pause or resume the project. Only the primary organizer can invite or remove co-organizers.

invite.copy: They'll see your invitation in their next sync. You can revoke any time before they accept.
invite.locked: Unlock this device with your passphrase before sending an invitation.

pending.title: Pending invitations
pending.revokeConfirmBody: They won't receive it on their next sync. You can invite them again later.

coorganizer.stepDownConfirmBody: You'll no longer be able to confirm task completions or edit this project. Your past confirmations remain.

coorg.capabilitiesTitle: What you can do as a co-organizer

capabilities.lifecycle: Launch, pause, resume, or complete the project.
capabilities.tasks: Add and edit tasks — one at a time or several at once.
capabilities.ordering: Set task order and sequencing (which tasks come before which).
capabilities.confirm: Confirm completed tasks. Each confirmation moves hours out of your own balance — you're signing as the helped party.
capabilities.stepDown: Step down from the role any time.

coorg.notIncluded: Inviting other co-organizers and archiving the project stay with the primary organizer.
coorg.acceptedPointer: You're a co-organizer now. See "What you can do" on the project page.

handoff.title: Pass the torch
handoff.intro: Transfer your primary role to a co-organizer. You'll stay as a co-organizer.

bulkTask.hint: Each line becomes a task with default settings. Edit individually after.

reinvite.title: Invite co-organizers again?
reinvite.intro: The original project had co-organizers. Fresh invitations go out for this new project — each person decides again. Nothing carries over automatically, and invitations expire after 14 days.
reinvite.candidateAria: Invite {{name}} to co-organize the new project
reinvite.skipHint: Uncheck anyone you'd rather not invite right now — you can always invite people later from the project page.
reinvite.partialToast: Clone created. Some invitations couldn't be sent — you can invite people from the new project page.

activityType.adopted_by_community: Stewardship handed on by community decision

workDays.heading: Upcoming work days
workDays.localLinkHint: Work days are community events connected to this project on this node only. Peer nodes see the event — never the project connection.

projects.adoptionBanner: The community is deciding on new stewardship for this project.

section.title: Community stewardship
section.intro: This project's organizer hasn't been active here in a while. The community can keep it alive by agreeing on a new primary organizer. Offering means taking on what the organizer role carries — invitations, archiving, handoffs.
section.rationalePlaceholder: What's your connection to this project, and what would you keep going?
section.notice: Your offer opens a community proposal. It stays open at least {{days}} days, and closes quietly if the current organizer is active again.

card.quietSince: No organizer activity on this project since {{when}}.
card.voidNote: If the current organizer is active again before this closes, it closes without effect — no questions asked.
card.execute: Close as passed and hand on stewardship
card.imHereHint: One tap closes this proposal. No explanation needed.

toast.filed: Your offer is open for the community to weigh in.
toast.executed: Stewardship handed on. The previous organizer stays on as co-organizer.
toast.voided: The organizer has been active again, so this closed without effect.

attention.title: The community is looking after "{{projectTitle}}"
attention.body: A proposal is open to add a new primary organizer while you're away. If you're around, one tap closes it.

closedReason.presence: Closed without effect — the organizer is active again.

myTasks.title: Tasks you're carrying
myTasks.subtitle: Every task you've claimed, across all projects, in one place.
myTasks.summaryOneProject: You're carrying {{count}} tasks in one project.
myTasks.emptyTitle: Nothing on your plate right now
myTasks.empty: When you claim a task in a community project it shows up here, so you can see your commitments without opening each project one by one.

myProjects.title: Projects you organize
myProjects.subtitle: Every project in your care — as organizer or co-organizer — and what's waiting on you, in one place.
myProjects.summaryOne: 1 project is in your care right now.
myProjects.summaryOther: {{count}} projects are in your care right now.
myProjects.pendingInvites_one: 1 co-organizer invitation waiting on a reply
myProjects.pendingInvites_other: {{count}} co-organizer invitations waiting on a reply
myProjects.emptyTitle: No projects in your care right now
myProjects.empty: When you start a project, or accept a co-organizer invitation, it shows up here — so you can see everything in your care without opening each one.

postForm.subtitleNeed: What would support look like right now?
postForm.subtitleOffer: What can you share with the community?
postForm.fieldHoursHint: One hour of help = one hour of credit, whatever the work.
postForm.placeholderTitleOffer: e.g. Extra soup and bread this week
postForm.placeholderDescription: Add any helpful details — timing, location zone, accessibility, etc.
postForm.errorNeedTitle: Give your post a short title so people know what it's about.
postForm.errorHoursPositive: Estimated hours must be a positive number.
postForm.errorExpiresInDays: Days must be a whole number greater than 0, or blank to never expire.
postForm.repostBanner: Editing a copy — the original post will be cancelled when you submit.
postForm.matchingNeeds: {{count}} active needs in this category — see them

postDetail.notFound: This post couldn't be found. It may have been cancelled.
postDetail.actionsWaiting: Waiting for someone to claim this.
postDetail.actionsClaimedBy: This post has been claimed. {{helper}} is helping {{helped}}.
postDetail.actionsCompleted: Completed. {{hours}} of credit flowed from {{helped}} to {{helper}}. Thank you both.
postDetail.dialogClaimNeedDescription: You're offering to help with {{hours}} of {{category}}. Credit flows to you once you've both confirmed completion.
postDetail.dialogClaimOfferDescription: You'd like to receive help for {{hours}}. Credit moves once you've both confirmed.
postDetail.dialogCompleteDescription: Confirm that the help actually happened. Credit moves once you've both confirmed. If something went wrong, you can flag it instead.
postDetail.dialogDisputeTitle: Flag this exchange for community review?
postDetail.dialogDisputeDescription: This surfaces the exchange on the Disputes page, where anyone in the community can weigh in — there are no admins. Credit doesn't move until it's resolved.
postDetail.dialogCancelDescription: Cancelled posts stay visible but can't be claimed.
postDetail.dialogReleaseDescription: This will reopen the post so someone else can step in.
postDetail.repostHint: This will cancel the original and create a fresh post.
postDetail.stillLooking: Still looking? Adding more detail or adjusting the hours estimate can help the right person find this. You can repost with changes below.
postDetail.stillOffering: Still available? Adjusting the timing or details can help match you with someone who needs this.

guidance.completed: Credits have transferred. Thank you both for strengthening the community.

claimed.party: {{name}} has claimed this. When the help is done, both of you confirm — credit moves when both sides have confirmed.

awaitingConfirmation.viewerOwes: {{name}} confirmed. Your confirmation completes the exchange and moves the credit.
awaitingConfirmation.viewerConfirmed: You've confirmed. Waiting on {{name}} — credit moves when both sides have confirmed.
awaitingConfirmation.autoConfirm_one: If they don't get to it, the node confirms automatically within about {{count}} day.
awaitingConfirmation.autoConfirm_other: If they don't get to it, the node confirms automatically within about {{count}} days.
awaitingConfirmation.thirdParty: Confirmed by one side; waiting on the other.

exchangeState.completed: This exchange is complete — credit has moved.
exchangeState.disputedBody: The community is discussing this exchange.

lockScreen.title: This device is locked
lockScreen.introWith: Enter your passphrase to unlock {{name}}'s identity on this device.
lockScreen.introNone: Enter your passphrase to unlock this device.
lockScreen.wrongPassphrase: That passphrase didn't match. Try again.
lockScreen.nothingToUnlock: No wrapped keys on this device — nothing to unlock. (If you're seeing this, reload the page.)
lockScreen.noRecoveryNote: Forgotten passphrases cannot be recovered — by design. If you've lost yours, the only path is Profile → Emergency → Hard purge and starting over with a fresh identity.

invite.wantsYou: {{name}} wants you in their mutual aid network.
invite.fingerprintReminder: Before accepting: confirm that the inviter-key fingerprint above matches what {{name}} shared with you in person or over a secure channel. A link that reached you via untrusted email is worth a double-check.
invite.welcome: Welcome in. Redirecting to the board…
invite.displayNameRequired: Choose a display name or pseudonym to continue.

errors.malformed: This invite link is missing or damaged. Ask the inviter to send a fresh one.
errors.expired: This invite has expired. Ask the inviter to issue a new one.
errors.bad_signature: This invite's signature didn't verify. Do not accept — it may have been tampered with.
errors.already_redeemed: Someone has already used this invite. Invites are single-use.
errors.self_redeem: You can't redeem an invite you issued yourself. Share the link with someone else.

trust.trustedTooltip: This member has at least two valid vouches.
trust.pendingTooltip: Fewer than two vouches so far. They can still post needs and offers.

trustedBy.title: Vouched for by
trustedBy.intro: These members signed a vouch (or invited this person into the community). Tap a name to see who they are.
trustedBy.empty: No one has vouched for this member yet.

first_exchange.label: First Exchange
first_exchange.description: You completed your first exchange — giving or receiving.

connector_5.label: Connector
connector_5.description: You've helped 5 different people in our community.

regular_4weeks.label: Regular
regular_4weeks.description: You've been active for 4 consecutive weeks — at least one exchange each week.

bridge_builder.label: Bridge Builder
bridge_builder.description: You fulfilled a need in a category no one had filled before.

seed_planter.label: Seed Planter
seed_planter.description: 3 people you invited have each completed an exchange.

listener.label: Listener
listener.description: You've completed 3 exchanges supporting someone emotionally.

weaver.label: Weaver
weaver.description: Your exchanges have connected members across 3 or more areas of our community.

groundbreaker.label: Groundbreaker
groundbreaker.description: You launched a community project that drew at least one contributor.

crew_member.label: Crew Member
crew_member.description: You completed 3 or more confirmed tasks across community projects.

momentum_maker.label: Momentum Maker
momentum_maker.description: A project you organized reached the halfway mark of its target hours.

keystone.label: Keystone
keystone.description: A project you organized was completed.

attention.title: Needs your attention
attention.intro: A few things are waiting on you. Tap to handle them when you have a moment.
attention.tapToConfirm: Tap to confirm and release the credits.
attention.tapToConfirmTask: Tap to confirm and credit the helper.

taskCheckIn.line: Still on “{{task}}” for {{project}}? You claimed it {{when}}.
taskCheckIn.hint: Capacity changes — release it if you need to. No record kept.

postClaimed.hint: A helper is on the way. Tap to see the post.

vouchReceived.hint: Each vouch brings you closer to trusted status.

projectPaused.line: {{project}} has been paused for a while.
projectPaused.hint: Resume it or mark it complete when ready.

coorgInvitation.hint: This commits your balance and identity to confirming work on the project. Take a look before you accept.
coorgInvitation.lockedToRespond: Unlock this device with your passphrase before responding.

accept.title: Accept co-organizer role for "{{project}}"
accept.meansConfirm: Confirming task completions — each confirmation moves hours out of your own balance.
accept.meansSign: Signing as a project organizer — records you sign in this role trace back to your identity in the project's federated history.
accept.meansVisible: Visible to the community — the project page lists you as a co-organizer on every peer's view.
accept.notObligation: No standing obligation — you can step back from the role yourself at any time.
accept.notDeputy: No authority over the primary — the primary organizer keeps handoff and removal authority. Co-organizers are peers, not deputies.

decline.confirmBody: No role is assigned. The organizer sees that you declined the next time they look at the project.

board.title: Community board
board.tagline: Post what you need. Offer what you can.

empty.needs: No needs match these filters yet. If you need help, post something — asking is never gated in a timebank.
empty.offers: No offers match these filters yet. What could you share with your community this week?

dashboard.title: Community dashboard
dashboard.tagline: The unit of progress is us, not me.

categoryBreakdown.title: Where help is flowing
categoryBreakdown.empty: No exchanges yet. Once the first one happens, you'll see the mix of care, labor, and support flowing through the community.

breadth.title: Breadth, not depth
breadth.intro: A glimpse of how widely help spreads — each bar is one member's reach across the community. Generosity spread widely, not piled deep.
breadth.empty: No exchanges yet. The first one will start the pattern.
breadth.moreNote: {{count}} more {{noun}} have reached at least one person — every name in the community would belong here if we listed them all.

reciprocity.title: Reciprocity
reciprocity.intro: Connections that flow both ways — A has helped B and B has helped A.
reciprocity.footnote: Not a score. Just a window into the shape of help across the community.

member.notFound: Member not found on this device.
member.vouchIntro: Vouching is a signed statement that this person is part of our community. Two distinct vouches (including invite redemption) mark a member as trusted.
member.vouchedJustNow: Vouched. Your signed statement is in the local ledger and (if a community node is configured) on its way there.
member.cannotVouchUntilTrusted: Vouching is enabled once you're trusted yourself. Your inviter's vouch is one; another community member can vouch for you to make it two.
member.alreadyFullyTrusted: This member is already trusted. Another vouch wouldn't change anything.
member.alreadyVouchedByYou: You've already vouched for this member.

settings.title: Settings
settings.intro: How Understoria behaves on this device. Changes here only affect this device — they don't sync across the community.

invitesPage.title: Your invites
invitesPage.intro: Invites you've issued. Open ones come first; you can copy, re-show the QR, or revoke any of them.

empty.title: No invites issued yet
empty.message: Generate your first invite from your Profile.

addDevice.title: Add another device
addDevice.subtitle: Use the same identity on a second device — phone, laptop, or both.

pairingLog.title: Paired devices
pairingLog.intro: Pair flows you've completed on this device — for your memory. This list lives only on this device.
pairingLog.rotateHint: To rotate your identity entirely (e.g. if a device is lost), see Emergency below.
pairingLog.destinationTitle: Times this device was authorized from elsewhere

balance.label: Your balance
balance.footerNote: Balances can go negative — asking is never gated.
balance.surplus: You've given a lot lately. Communities stay healthy when helpers ask too — what would help you?
balance.neutral: Your balance is right at your starting seed. That's a fine place to be.
balance.receiving: You've been receiving — that's what seed credits are for. Ask for what you need.
balance.pendingExplainerAuto_one: Credit moves when both sides confirm an exchange. If a partner doesn't get to it, the community node confirms for them within about {{count}} day — nothing is stuck.
balance.pendingExplainerAuto_other: Credit moves when both sides confirm an exchange. If a partner doesn't get to it, the community node confirms for them within about {{count}} days — nothing is stuck.
balance.pendingExplainerManual: Credit moves when both sides confirm an exchange. Nothing is stuck — it lands the moment the second confirmation happens.
balance.projectOutflowLine: Includes {{hours}} you moved to helpers on behalf of {{project}}.
balance.projectOutflowLineMore_one: Includes {{hours}} you moved to helpers on behalf of {{project}} and {{count}} other project.
balance.projectOutflowLineMore_other: Includes {{hours}} you moved to helpers on behalf of {{project}} and {{count}} other projects.

about.title: About you
about.avatarNote: This little plant is your identity, drawn from your public key. Everyone with the same key sees the same plant. It can't be changed — that's how others recognize you across the app.
about.name: Display name (pseudonym is fine)
about.availabilitySubhead: Optional. Pick what fits, or leave empty — 'Ask me anytime' is fine too.

rolesEarned.title: Your community roles
rolesEarned.empty: No named roles yet. They show up as you participate — not as trophies, but as ways of naming the shapes your contributions take.

history.title: Your exchange history
history.empty: Nothing here yet. When you give or receive help, each exchange shows up with a signed record you can verify.
history.flagShort: Very short exchange — surfaced for community review.
history.flagReciprocal: Repeated reciprocal exchange with the same person — surfaced for community review.

memberSwitcher.title: Switch member (local dev)
memberSwitcher.note: In a real deployment each device holds one identity. This switcher exists so you can walk through the full exchange flow yourself while testing.

data.title: Data & privacy
data.intro: Everything you see is stored locally on this device. No server. No analytics. No account to sign up for.

learn.title: Learn
learn.intro: The welcome flow, common questions, a condensed member guide, and prompts for study groups. Everything here works offline.
learn.opsecIntro: Mutual aid work carries real risks. This short guide covers the basics of keeping yourself and your community safe. Read it once; come back when something changes.
learn.principlesIntro: Every design choice in Understoria has a reason. These principles explain why the app works the way it does, with examples of what happened when other projects tried it differently.

communitySettings.title: Community settings
communitySettings.intro: Thresholds the community can tune to fit its own context. Defaults work for most groups.
communitySettings.bootstrapNote: These changes take effect immediately for everyone on this device. Once the in-app proposal system ships, edits here will route through a community vote instead.
communitySettings.proposeLink: Or propose a change for the community to discuss →

dailyLimit.label: Daily helper limit
dailyLimit.hint: The most exchanges any single helper can complete in 24 hours. A hard stop, not a guideline.

shortExchange.label: Short-exchange flag threshold (hours)
shortExchange.hint: Exchanges shorter than this get an advisory flag for community review. Not blocked, not punished — just surfaced.

reciprocal.label: Reciprocal-pattern threshold
reciprocal.hint: When the same two members exchange this many times in 30 days, the latest exchange gets an advisory flag.

taskCheckInDays.label: Task check-in (days)
taskCheckInDays.hint: After this many days, the claimer of a project task gets a private 'still on it?' prompt in their attention list. They can release without anyone seeing.

taskNeedsHelpDays.label: Task 'could use more hands' (days)
taskNeedsHelpDays.hint: After this many days, a claimed task is publicly marked as needing more hands. Framed at the task, not the person. Must be at least the check-in threshold.

taskCheckInGraceDays.label: Task check-in grace (days)
taskCheckInGraceDays.hint: After the claimer acknowledges the private nudge, suppress the public 'could use more hands' chip for this many days. Lets a responsive claimer keep the task out of community signalling. Keep small (1-3) so prolonged silence still surfaces.

deliberationDays.label: Proposal deliberation period (days)
deliberationDays.hint: Minimum days a proposal must stay open before it can auto-pass on consensus. Prevents rush votes.

minAffirms.label: Minimum affirm votes
minAffirms.hint: Distinct affirm votes required for a proposal to auto-pass. Even with deliberation done, a proposal that only one person has affirmed shouldn't pass silently.

inviteOnly.label: Invite-only onboarding
inviteOnly.helpText: When enabled, visitors to /welcome see an invite-only landing. The first member to onboard on a fresh node is the exception (bootstrap).

language.title: Language
language.intro: Choose how Understoria speaks to you on this device. More languages and translations of the rest of the app are coming as the i18n migration proceeds.

appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.intro: Light or dark. Match system follows your device — useful if you've set your OS to switch by time of day.
appearance.textSizeIntro: Multiplies with your device's text size — if you've made it larger system-wide, the larger options here stack on top.
appearance.autoSublabel: On this screen, Auto shows {{resolved}}. Resize the window to see it switch.
appearance.densityIntro: Compact trims card padding so denser screens fit more above the fold. Touch targets stay the same size.

node.title: Community node
node.intro: Optionally mirror your finalized exchanges to a community node so they show up in the community-wide ledger. The node verifies every signature; it never stores your private key.
node.enableLabel: Mirror finalized exchanges to this node

consent.title: Send your exchange records to this server?
consent.body: Turning on mirroring sends every exchange you take part in to this server — including the other person's public key, the signature, category, hours, and time. A server you don't control can use this to map who-helps-whom across your community. Only enable this for a node your community runs and trusts.

invites.title: Invites you've issued
invites.intro: A new member needs two vouches to become trusted. Your invite counts as the first — someone else will need to vouch for them after they join.
invites.empty: You haven't invited anyone yet. When you do, the invite link will show up here so you can re-share it or revoke it.
invites.shareTitle: Share this link with one person
invites.shareNote: The link is single-use and expires in 14 days. If it leaks before redemption, revoke it below.
invites.revealedHint: Anyone in camera view can read this. Hide it again when you're done.

shareSheet.title: Share this invite
shareSheet.intro: Show the QR code in person, or send the link with one person you trust. Anyone with this link can join — share it carefully.
shareSheet.qrAriaLabel: QR code containing the invite link
shareSheet.qrFallback: Couldn't draw the QR code. Use the link below instead.
shareSheet.shareText: I'm inviting you to join my community on Understoria.
shareSheet.statusFailed: Couldn't share or copy. Select the link and copy it manually.

cameraGate.title: Look around before you show this
cameraGate.body: Security cameras and webcams can read QR codes from across a room. Once it's on screen, anyone in camera view can save it and use it to join your community themselves.
cameraGate.followup: This prompt comes up every time — your surroundings can change between one share and the next.
cameraGate.shareWithoutShowing: Send the link without showing it
cameraGate.notAvailable: Your browser can't share or copy without showing the link. Tap "Show the invite" and copy it by hand instead.
cameraGate.statusCopied: Link copied to your clipboard. Paste it into Signal, Messages, or wherever you want to send it — the link is not on screen.
cameraGate.statusFailed: Your browser blocked the share and the clipboard. Tap "Show the invite" to copy it by hand.

security.title: Security
security.summaryProtected: Your secret keys on this device are wrapped with a passphrase-derived key. Unlock is required at every launch.
security.summaryUnprotected: Your secret keys are stored in plaintext on this device. Enable passphrase protection before leaving the device alone in any hostile environment.
security.passphraseHint: At least 8 characters. A four-word pass-phrase from a password manager is ideal. There is no recovery — we cannot get this back for you.
security.disableWarn: This will rewrite your wrapped secret keys as plaintext on this device. Only do this if you're sure the device is safe.
security.successEnable: Passphrase protection enabled. Write your passphrase down somewhere safe — it can't be recovered.
security.successChange: Passphrase changed. Use the new one next time you unlock.
security.successDisable: Passphrase protection disabled. Secret keys are stored in plaintext on this device again.
security.errorMismatchNew: The two new passphrases didn't match.

emergency.title: Emergency
emergency.intro: Two panic options for when a device is at risk. Neither contacts a server; both happen entirely on this device.
emergency.softBullet: Soft purge — strips every identifying text field (names, descriptions, areas, skills) while keeping the signed exchange ledger and your keypair. Useful if a device will briefly be handled by a hostile party.
emergency.hardBullet: Hard purge — wipes every table including private keys, rotates to a fresh node identity. Unrecoverable. The page will reload afterward.
emergency.softConfirmDescription: Identifying text on every member and post will be blanked out. Signed exchange records and your keypair will be preserved. This is not reversible.
emergency.hardConfirmDescription: Every table will be wiped — including your private keys. A fresh node identity will be generated and the app will reload. There is no undo.

help.title: Common questions
help.subtitle: Quick answers for the things members hit while using the app. Looking for the bigger picture? Check the member guide or revisit the welcome from your profile.
help.footer: Didn't find your question? Most of what isn't here is a community conversation, not an app feature — talk to a member you trust.

disputes.title: Disputes
disputes.subtitle: Exchanges someone has flagged for the community to review. Anyone can see this list — there are no admins. The two parties already see the flag on the post itself; this is where it surfaces to everyone else.
disputes.empty: Nothing flagged right now. When someone flags an exchange, it shows here so the community can see and respond.
disputes.footer: Each dispute also shows up in Decisions, where the community can vote on how to respond. Resolution closes the dispute in both places.

entry.title: Community disputes
entry.descriptionNone: No exchanges are flagged right now.
entry.descriptionOne: {{count}} exchange has been flagged for community review.
entry.descriptionOther: {{count}} exchanges have been flagged for community review.

proposals.title: Proposals
proposals.subtitle: Community decisions in progress. Anyone can start one; anyone can record an outcome once the community has agreed. Voting in-app comes later — for now this surface tracks the decisions you made elsewhere.
proposals.footer: Voting and automatic close are still to come. For now the community reaches decisions on whatever channel it uses, and any member can record the outcome here so the historical record reflects reality.
proposals.outcomeReasonPlaceholder: A short note about how the community got here.
proposals.recordOutcomeHint: Community decided already? Record the outcome:
proposals.consensusReached: Consensus reached: {{affirms}} affirm, {{blocks}} blocks. Any member can close this proposal.

closedReason.consensus: Closed by member action after consensus conditions were met.

reversibility.easyTooltip: Another proposal can undo this. Most config tweaks land here.
reversibility.easyHint: Another proposal can undo this. Most config tweaks land here.
reversibility.moderateTooltip: Takes effort to reverse. Worth a pause before passing.
reversibility.moderateHint: Takes effort to reverse. Worth a pause before passing.
reversibility.hardTooltip: Reversal would require rebuilding something. Impact reflection encouraged.
reversibility.hardHint: Reversal would require rebuilding something. Impact reflection is encouraged for hard-tier proposals.

entry.title: Community proposals
entry.descriptionOne: {{count}} proposal open for the community to discuss.
entry.descriptionOther: {{count}} proposals open for the community to discuss.

proposals.errorTitleRequired: Give the proposal a short title.
proposals.errorPositiveInteger: Must be a whole number greater than 0.

eligibility.waitDeliberation: Auto-passes {{when}} if no blocks land before then.
eligibility.waitAffirms: Needs {{need}} affirms to auto-pass on consensus; has {{have}} so far.
eligibility.blocked: Held by {{count}} block — needs to be resolved before this can move forward.

vote.blockReasonPlaceholder: What needs to be resolved before this can move forward?

toast.created: Proposal started. Share it with your community on your usual channel.

new.title: Start a proposal
new.subtitle: Propose a change to your community's node configuration. The proposal stays open until someone records the outcome — that step is manual for now while we get a feel for what proposals actually look like.
new.descriptionPlaceholder: What problem does this solve? Any concerns? How should the community weigh in?
new.configIntro: Edit any of the three thresholds. Unchanged fields stay at their current value.
new.tierIntro: How hard would this be to undo? Defaults to easy because config can be changed back by another proposal — bump to moderate or hard if the cultural impact is bigger than the technical reversal.
new.impactIntro: Hard-to-reverse decisions deserve a pause. All four prompts are required for hard-tier proposals — the point is to slow down and think through each dimension before something that's harder to take back.
new.impactFooter: These notes will be visible to your community alongside the proposal.

impact.yearOneLabel: What changes in the first year?
impact.yearOnePlaceholder: Concrete effects you can already see.
impact.fiveYearPlaceholder: What does this look like once it's settled in?
impact.reversalPathLabel: How would this be undone if it's wrong?
impact.reversalPathPlaceholder: What's the path back? Who'd have to agree?
impact.vulnerableImpactLabel: Who's most affected — especially the most vulnerable members?
impact.vulnerableImpactPlaceholder: Who carries the weight if this goes badly?

customMilestones.title: Custom milestones
customMilestones.intro: These add to the baseline hours, exchanges, and members milestones with thresholds meaningful to your community. They appear on the Dashboard alongside the baselines.
customMilestones.labelHint: What to celebrate when this is reached
customMilestones.emptyMessage: No custom milestones yet. Add ones meaningful to your community.

validation.duplicate: A milestone with this type and threshold already exists.
validation.labelRequired: Give the milestone a label so members know what it celebrates.
validation.threshold: Threshold must be a positive whole number.

welcome.nextSteps: Next: browse what neighbors need, or post an offer of your own.
welcome.pairDeviceLink: Already have your identity on another device? Pair this one →

timebank.title: This is a timebank
timebank.body: Help is measured in hours. One hour of your help is worth one hour of anyone's.
timebank.bodyMore: No prices. No ratings. Every kind of help counts the same.

credit.title: You start with credit
credit.body: Everyone begins with 5 hours. You can ask for help before you give any.
credit.bodyMore: Asking is normal. It's how neighbors help each other.

identity.title: You hold your own key
identity.body: No email, no password. You have a private key that only you hold.
identity.bodyMore: No company owns it, so no one can lock you out. Keep your key safe.

community.title: The community decides
community.body: There are no bosses here. People make choices together.
community.bodyMore: If something goes wrong, it starts with talking, not banning.

projects.title: Some help needs a group
projects.body: Big efforts, like a tool library, take more than one favor. A project is a shared goal many people work on.
projects.bodyMore: Look for one already going before you start a new one.

profileSetup.title: A little about you
profileSetup.intro: All optional — these help others find you when they need someone nearby or with a particular skill. You can change any of it later in Profile.
profileSetup.hint: Nothing here is private to the network — only share what you'd share with a neighbor.

inviteOnly.title: Understoria is invite-only
inviteOnly.body1: This community runs on personal invitations from existing members.
inviteOnly.body2: If a member sent you an invitation link, follow that link instead. If not, ask a member to send one.

commits.cannotReach: {{name}} can no longer DM you, claim your posts, vouch for you, or RSVP to your events.
commits.youWontSee: You won't see their posts, comments, vouches, or events.
commits.governanceExtra: Their proposals, votes, and dispute comments will also be hidden from your view. They will still appear to every other member.

notCommits.dispute: This does not file a dispute or notify moderators. To raise a community concern, see Profile → Disputes.
notCommits.deletion: This does not delete their signed records from the federation.
notCommits.otherNodes: This does not block them on other nodes.

confirm.notePlaceholder: Private note (optional, only you see this)
confirm.hideGovernanceCheckbox: Also hide their proposals, votes, and dispute comments from me.
confirm.hideGovernanceWhy: By default, you still see their governance contributions. Hiding them here is a personal choice — their voice still reaches every other member of the community.

unblock.confirmBody: They'll be able to message you, claim your posts, vouch for you, and RSVP to your events again. Existing history (your previous block of them) stays in your local history.

settings.sectionIntro: Your personal block list. This stays on this device — it never federates, and no other member can see it.
settings.activeEmpty: You haven't blocked anyone yet. To block someone, open a conversation with them and tap the menu in the conversation header.
settings.historyEmpty: No history yet. When you unblock someone, they'll appear here as a memory aid.
settings.hideGovernanceToggle: Hide their governance contributions from me
settings.clearHistoryConfirmBody: This deletes every row in your previously-blocked memory aid. Active blocks are unaffected. This cannot be undone.
settings.crossDeviceWarning: Blocks reach a newly-paired device when you pair it. Devices you paired before creating a block need to be re-paired to receive it.

error.noteTooLong: Note must be 500 characters or fewer.
